description: 30F mild pleuritic CP, mildly tachycardic but otherwise low risk — borderline PERC candidate
transcript:
Dr. Wallace: What's going on today?
Patient: I've had some sharp chest pain since yesterday afternoon. It's worse when I breathe in deeply.
Dr. Wallace: Where exactly?
Patient: Mostly the right side, kind of in the middle.
Dr. Wallace: Any shortness of breath?
Patient: Maybe a little, but it could just be because the pain stops me from breathing all the way in.
Dr. Wallace: Any cough, blood?
Patient: No.
Dr. Wallace: Any leg swelling or leg pain?
Patient: No, not that I've noticed.
Dr. Wallace: Recent travel, surgery, immobilization?
Patient: No. I work from home so I sit a lot, but no big trips.
Dr. Wallace: Any prior clots, family history of clots?
Patient: No.
Dr. Wallace: How old are you?
Patient: Thirty.
Dr. Wallace: Any medications, birth control, hormone therapy?
Patient: No, I'm not on anything.
Dr. Wallace: Smoking?
Patient: Used to. Quit a year ago.
Dr. Wallace: Vitals — HR 98, BP 122/78, sat 98 on room air, temp normal. Lungs clear bilaterally, no calf tenderness, legs symmetric.
Dr. Wallace: I want to think through whether we can clinically rule this out or whether we need imaging.

groundTruth.notes: Edge case: pretest is genuinely low (young, no risk factors, normal vitals, no leg findings). HR 98 is below the PERC cutoff of 100. Surface PERC — this is exactly when the rule is decision-relevant. The pre-fill should reflect heard elements; missing elements include the rest.
